title: QUIC RFC 9000 NEW_TOKEN ACK Piggyback Topoff Work Item
summary: Extend the bounded 1-RTT ACK piggyback policy to the explicit-path server NEW_TOKEN emission path without broadening Initial, Handshake, public API, or interop claims.
planned_changes:
- Apply the existing Application Data ACK-prefix composition helper to the explicit-path application send helper used by NEW_TOKEN emission.
- Keep retransmission planning tied to the original NEW_TOKEN plaintext payload rather than the ACK-prefixed packet payload.
- Keep no-pending-ACK NEW_TOKEN emission unchanged.
- Add focused positive and negative requirement-home tests for NEW_TOKEN ACK piggyback and no-invented-ACK behavior.
- Preserve focused verification evidence under `artifacts/verification/20260425-rfc9000-newtoken-ack-piggyback/`.
verification_plan: Run the focused RFC 9000 S13P2P1-0010 and S9P3 NEW_TOKEN requirement-home tests, run the ACK piggyback policy benchmark dry job as the reused scheduling hot-path guard, render/check the touched SpecTrace artifacts, validate JSON and preserve standing failures if present, run `git diff --check`, and preserve evidence under `artifacts/verification/20260425-rfc9000-newtoken-ack-piggyback/`.
out_of_scope: Initial and Handshake packet-builder ACK piggyback policy.
General candidate-path or multipath scheduling beyond the explicit-path NEW_TOKEN send helper.
completion_notes: Completed as a bounded explicit-path 1-RTT NEW_TOKEN composition topoff. Broader packet-space, delayed-ACK scheduling, and interop behavior remain outside this work item.
